-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fbcl","title":"EPIC: Landing Page & SEO","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5cy.

## Overview
Marketing landing page and SEO optimization for organic discovery.

## Landing Page (/)

### Hero Section
- Headline: Value proposition
- Subheadline: Brief description
- CTA buttons: "Get Started Free" / "View Pricing"
- Hero image/animation showing product

### Features Section
- Grid of key features with icons
- Swap Meet / Community
- Prompt Packs
- Skills
- Collections
- CLI Integration
- Analytics

### How It Works
- Step-by-step visual guide
- 1. Sign up with Google
- 2. Browse or create prompts
- 3. Organize and share

### Social Proof
- Testimonials (when we have them)
- User count / prompt count stats
- "As seen in" logos (if applicable)

### Pricing Preview
- Simple free vs premium comparison
- Link to full /pricing page

### Footer CTA
- Final call to action
- Email signup for updates (optional)

## SEO Optimization

### Meta Tags
- Dynamic title and description per page
- Open Graph tags for social sharing
- Twitter Card tags
- Canonical URLs

### Dynamic OG Images
- Generate OG images for shared prompts
- Include title, author, rating
- Branded template

### Technical SEO
- Sitemap.xml generation
- robots.txt configuration
- Schema.org structured data
- Fast page loads (Core Web Vitals)

## Pages to Create
- / (landing)
- /pricing (detailed pricing)
- /about (optional)
- /changelog (what's new)

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","title":"Implement admin role permissions system","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-wbx0. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create granular role-based access control for admin functions.

## Roles
- Super Admin: Full access, can manage other admins
- Admin: Full content and user management
- Moderator: Content review, user warnings
- Support: Read-only, can escalate

## Permissions
```typescript
const PERMISSIONS = {
  // User management
  'users.view': ['moderator', 'support', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'users.edit': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  'users.suspend': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  'users.delete': ['super_admin'],
  
  // Content moderation
  'content.view_reported': ['moderator', 'support', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'content.moderate': ['moderator', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'content.delete': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  
  // Billing
  'billing.view': ['support', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'billing.refund': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  
  // Admin management
  'admins.view': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  'admins.manage': ['super_admin'],
};
```

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const adminRoles = pgTable('admin_roles', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  role: text('role', { enum: ['super_admin', 'admin', 'moderator', 'support'] }),
  grantedBy: uuid('granted_by').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
});
```

## UI
- Role indicator in admin header
- Permission-gated UI elements
- Role management page (super admin)

## Success Criteria
- Proper permission enforcement
- Clear role boundaries
- Audit log for role changes
- API and UI consistent","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:06.423006677-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:34:24.180745242-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:36.197636282-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fo8i","title":"Implement moderation appeal process","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-u8t6.

## Task
Allow users to appeal moderation decisions.

## Why This Matters
- Fairness to users
- Catch false positives
- Build trust
- Legal protection

## Appeal Process

### 1. Submit Appeal
- From suspension/removal notice
- Explain why decision wrong
- Provide evidence
- One appeal per action

### 2. Review Queue
- Admin reviews appeals
- See original violation
- See user explanation
- Make decision

### 3. Outcomes
- Upheld (keep action)
- Overturned (reverse action)
- Modified (reduce severity)

### 4. Communication
- Notify user of outcome
- Explain reasoning
- Final decision (no re-appeal)

## Database
```typescript
export const moderationAppeals = pgTable('moderation_appeals', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // What is being appealed
  moderationActionId: uuid('moderation_action_id').notNull(),
  actionType: text('action_type').notNull(), // content_removed, account_suspended, etc.
  
  // Appellant
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // Appeal content
  reason: text('reason').notNull(),
  evidence: text('evidence'),
  
  // Status
  status: text('status').default('pending'), // pending, reviewing, upheld, overturned, modified
  
  // Review
  reviewedBy: uuid('reviewed_by').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  reviewedAt: timestamp('reviewed_at'),
  reviewNotes: text('review_notes'),
  outcome: text('outcome'),
  
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## UI Components
- AppealForm - Submit appeal
- AppealStatus - Track appeal status
- AppealQueue - Admin queue
- AppealReview - Admin review panel

## API Endpoints
- POST /api/appeals - Submit appeal
- GET /api/appeals/[id] - Get appeal status
- GET /api/admin/appeals - List appeals (admin)
- PATCH /api/admin/appeals/[id] - Resolve appeal (admin)

## Limits
- 1 appeal per moderation action
- 7 days to submit appeal
- Admin has 14 days to review
- No re-appeals after decision

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/appeals.ts
- src/app/api/appeals/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/appeals/[id]/page.tsx
- src/app/(app)/admin/appeals/page.tsx
- src/components/moderation/appeal-form.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Appeals can be submitted
- Admin can review efficiently
- Users notified of outcomes
- Process is fair and documented

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-lx09","title":"EPIC: Legal Pages & Compliance","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ynx.

## Overview
Legal pages, compliance requirements, and user-facing policies.

## Required Pages

### 1. Terms of Service (/terms)
- User obligations
- Content ownership and licensing
- Subscription terms
- Limitation of liability
- Termination conditions

### 2. Privacy Policy (/privacy)
- Data we collect
- How we use it
- Third-party services (Stripe, Supabase, analytics)
- User rights (access, deletion, export)
- Cookie policy
- Contact information

### 3. Community Guidelines (/guidelines)
- Acceptable content for Swap Meet
- Prohibited content
- Enforcement actions
- Reporting process
- Appeal process

### 4. Cookie Consent (if required)
- Cookie banner component
- Cookie preferences modal
- Store consent in localStorage + backend

## GDPR Compliance

### Data Export
- API endpoint to generate data export
- Background job to compile all user data
- Email notification when ready
- Download link with expiration

### Account Deletion
- Soft delete with 30-day grace period
- Hard delete after grace period
- Cancel subscription on deletion request
- Delete all user content
- Anonymize analytics data

## Implementation
- MDX pages for legal content (easy to update)
- Version tracking for legal documents
- "Accept updated terms" flow when terms change

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-m61m","title":"Configure security headers and CSP","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-vxw. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Configure security headers and Content Security Policy for production.

## Security Headers

### Required Headers
```typescript
// next.config.ts headers
{
  'X-DNS-Prefetch-Control': 'on',
  'Strict-Transport-Security': 'max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload',
  'X-Frame-Options': 'SAMEORIGIN',
  'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff',
  'Referrer-Policy': 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin',
  'Permissions-Policy': 'camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()',
}
```

### Content Security Policy
```
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' https://js.stripe.com;
style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';
img-src 'self' data: https: blob:;
font-src 'self' data:;
connect-src 'self' https://*.supabase.co https://api.stripe.com;
frame-src https://js.stripe.com https://hooks.stripe.com;
object-src 'none';
base-uri 'self';
form-action 'self';
```

### CSP Nonce (for inline scripts)
- Generate nonce per request
- Apply to script tags
- Pass via middleware

## Implementation

### next.config.ts
- Add security headers
- Configure CSP

### proxy.ts (middleware)
- Generate CSP nonce
- Add to response headers

### Verification
- Security headers scan
- CSP validation
- No console errors

## Files to Modify
- next.config.ts
- src/proxy.ts

## Success Criteria
- All security headers present
- CSP blocks unauthorized sources
- No functionality broken
- A+ on securityheaders.com

## CSP Hardening Notes
- Prefer nonce-based script-src; avoid unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval wherever possible.
- Add frame-ancestors 'self' to prevent clickjacking (keeps Stripe frames on frame-src only).
- Add report-to or report-uri for CSP violation reporting (route to Sentry or log endpoint).
- Include upgrade-insecure-requests and block-all-mixed-content in production.
- Validate Supabase, Stripe, and asset domains explicitly (no wildcard unless needed).
